title: You Don't Need React Portals For Modals Anymore (react-call)
summary: react-call is a lightweight React utility that enables calling components as asynchronous functions, replacing the need for React Portals when building modals. It uses Root and Stack, Upsert Singletons, and Mutation Flow patterns to handle layout isolation and state management without context providers or complex state tracking.
writeup: This Better Stack video introduces react-call, a lightweight React utility that lets components be called as asynchronous functions, removing the need for React Portals when building modals. It covers the library's Root and Stack components, Upsert Singletons, and Mutation Flow patterns, which handle layout isolation and state management without context providers or manual state tracking. The takeaway: modal and dialog flows can be triggered imperatively, with the promise-based call pattern replacing the boilerplate that Portals and context typically require.
